title: From documents to a RAG corpus in R
text: doclingr turns messy documents — PDF, DOCX, PPTX, HTML, images — structured, AI-ready data. wraps Docling Python library reticulate, giving layout-aware parsing, table extraction retrieval-ready chunking small, tidy R API. vignette walks full path: document → structure → tables → chunks → embeddings, .e. everything need stand retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) corpus R.

title: From documents to a RAG corpus in R
text: doclingr needs Docling Python package. Install managed environment, restart R:
code: library(doclingr)  install_docling()      # creates an "r-docling" Python environment # ...restart R... docling_available()    # TRUE once the backend is ready

title: From documents to a RAG corpus in R
text: docling_convert() runs Docling’s understanding pipeline file path URL returns lightweight handle: Tune pipeline need . OCR accurate table model cost time; turn born-digital documents large batches:
code: doc <- docling_convert("https://arxiv.org/pdf/2408.09869") doc #> <docling_document> #> source: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2408.09869 #> pages: 9 #> tables: 5 #> figures: 3 doc <- docling_convert(   "report.pdf",   ocr = FALSE,           # skip OCR for born-digital PDFs   table_mode = "fast",   # "accurate" (default) or "fast"   device = "mps"         # "auto", "cpu", "cuda", "mps" )  # Convert many sources in one batch docs <- docling_convert(c("a.pdf", "b.docx", "c.html"))

title: From documents to a RAG corpus in R
text: Render understood document format downstream tools expect:
code: as_markdown(doc)   # layout-aware Markdown as_text(doc)       # plain text as_html(doc)       # HTML as_json(doc)       # structured DoclingDocument as a nested R list as_doctags(doc)    # Docling's DocTags representation

title: From documents to a RAG corpus in R
text: Every detected table comes back tibble, document order:
code: tables <- docling_tables(doc) length(tables) tables[[1]] #> # A tibble: 12 x 4 #>    Method     Recall Precision  F1 #>    <chr>       <chr>     <chr> <chr> #>  1 Baseline    0.81      0.78  0.79 #>  ...

title: From documents to a RAG corpus in R
text: Pull figure captions pages, optionally save images (requires images = TRUE conversion time):
code: doc <- docling_convert("paper.pdf", images = TRUE) figs <- docling_figures(doc, image_dir = "figures") figs #> # A tibble: 3 x 4 #>   figure_id caption                 page image_path #>       <int> <chr>                  <int> <chr> #> 1         1 "Figure 1: pipeline ..."   2 figures/figure-001.png #> ...

title: From documents to a RAG corpus in R
text: docling_chunk() splits document context-rich chunks. default hybrid chunker token-aware: match tokenizer embedding model set budget chunks fit model’s context. chunk’s text contextualized — enriched heading path table context — form typically embed. unmodified text kept raw_text.
code: chunks <- docling_chunk(   doc,   tokenizer = "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5",   max_tokens = 512 ) chunks #> # A tibble: 84 x 7 #>    chunk_id text         raw_text     n_chars headings   pages   n_doc_items #>       <int> <chr>        <chr>          <int> <list>     <list>        <int> #>  1        1 "Docling: ..." "Docling..."     412 <chr [2]>  <int [1]>         3 #>  ...

title: From documents to a RAG corpus in R
text: doclingr deliberately provider-agnostic embeddings: supply function maps character vector vectors, docling_embed() handles batching tidy assembly. sketch OpenAI-style API: point corpus tidy table chunks headings, pages embeddings — ready write vector store, database, -memory nearest-neighbor index RAG.
code: embed_api <- function(texts) {   # Call your embedding endpoint; return a matrix with one row per text.   # e.g. httr2 -> a list of vectors, or a matrix. }  corpus <- doc |>   docling_chunk(tokenizer = "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5", max_tokens = 512) |>   docling_embed(embed_api, batch_size = 64)  corpus #> # ... your chunks plus `embedding` (list-column) and `n_dim`

title: From documents to a RAG corpus in R
text: Use as_json(doc) need full structural detail Docling captured. Persist corpus (example arrow::write_parquet()) avoid re-converting re-embedding. See Docling documentation breadth supported formats pipeline options.

title: Conversion options and performance
text: doclingr wraps Docling Python library reticulate. Install backend managed environment, restart R: deep-learning models (layout, tables, OCR) download first conversion cached afterwards. control stored, set Hugging Face cache first conversion:
code: library(doclingr)  install_docling()         # creates an "r-docling" virtualenv # ...restart R... docling_available()       # TRUE Sys.setenv(HF_HOME = "~/.cache/doclingr-models")

title: Conversion options and performance
text: OCR reads text scanned pages images. default. born-digital PDFs (exported Word, LaTeX, etc.) text layer already present, turning OCR large, safe speed-: Leave OCR scans, photographs documents, anything text “painted” image.
code: doc <- docling_convert("born-digital.pdf", ocr = FALSE)

title: Conversion options and performance
code: # Best structure (default) -- complex, spanning, nested tables docling_convert("report.pdf", table_mode = "accurate")  # Quicker -- clean grids, large batches docling_convert("report.pdf", table_mode = "fast")

title: Conversion options and performance
text: Pick device models run , optionally CPU thread count: device = "auto" (default) lets Docling choose.
code: docling_convert("report.pdf", device = "mps")               # Apple Silicon docling_convert("report.pdf", device = "cuda")              # NVIDIA GPU docling_convert("report.pdf", device = "cpu", num_threads = 8)

title: Conversion options and performance
text: default images retained, keeps results small. Ask want save figures work page images: images_scale = 2 renders roughly twice 72 DPI; raise crisper figure exports cost memory.
code: doc  <- docling_convert("paper.pdf", images = TRUE, images_scale = 2) figs <- docling_figures(doc, image_dir = "figures") figs

title: Conversion options and performance
text: Pass vector sources convert one batch; result named list documents:
code: docs <- docling_convert(   c("a.pdf", "b.docx", "c.html"),   ocr        = FALSE,   table_mode = "fast" )  length(docs) docs[["a.pdf"]]

title: Conversion options and performance
text: large pile born-digital reports mostly care text tables: chunk embed shown vignette("rag").
code: docs <- docling_convert(   list.files("reports", pattern = "[.]pdf$", full.names = TRUE),   ocr        = FALSE,      # no scans   table_mode = "fast",     # clean grids   device     = "auto" )

title: Building a RAG pipeline
text: Chunk size respect context window embedding model use. docling_chunk() token-aware: point tokenizer embedder max_tokens budget measured right units. chunk’s text contextualized – prefixed heading path table context – form embed. unmodified passage kept raw_text, headings/pages let cite sources later.
code: library(doclingr)  doc <- docling_convert("paper.pdf")  chunks <- docling_chunk(   doc,   tokenizer  = "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5",   max_tokens = 512 ) chunks

title: Building a RAG pipeline
text: doclingr lock embedding provider. supply function maps character vector vectors; docling_embed() handles batching tidy assembly. sketch HTTP embeddings API: local model works just well – anything returns matrix list numeric vectors:
code: embed_api <- function(texts) {   # POST `texts` to your endpoint and return a matrix: one row per text.   # e.g. with httr2:   resp <- httr2::request("https://api.example.com/v1/embeddings") |>     httr2::req_headers(Authorization = paste("Bearer", Sys.getenv("EMBED_KEY"))) |>     httr2::req_body_json(list(model = "bge-small", input = texts)) |>     httr2::req_perform()   do.call(rbind, lapply(httr2::resp_body_json(resp)$data, \(d) unlist(d$embedding))) }  corpus <- docling_embed(chunks, embed_api, batch_size = 64) corpus # Using a sentence-transformers model through reticulate st <- reticulate::import("sentence_transformers") model <- st$SentenceTransformer("BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5") embed_local <- function(texts) model$encode(texts)  corpus <- docling_embed(chunks, embed_local, batch_size = 64)

title: Building a RAG pipeline
text: embeddings matrix, retrieval plain R. Embed query way, rank chunks cosine similarity: larger corpora, push embeddings dedicated vector store instead -memory matrix.
code: emb <- do.call(rbind, corpus$embedding)  cosine_top <- function(query, k = 5) {   q <- as.numeric(embed_api(query))   sims <- as.numeric(emb %*% q) /     (sqrt(rowSums(emb^2)) * sqrt(sum(q^2)))   corpus[order(sims, decreasing = TRUE)[seq_len(k)], c("text", "headings", "pages")] }  cosine_top("What datasets were used for evaluation?")

title: Building a RAG pipeline
text: Converting embedding expensive steps. Save corpus pay :
code: arrow::write_parquet(corpus, "corpus.parquet") # later: corpus <- arrow::read_parquet("corpus.parquet")

title: Building a RAG pipeline
text: docling_convert() accepts vector sources converts one batch. Combine chunk/embed steps build corpus folder: corpus – chunk text, headings, pages, source embeddings one tidy table – everything need power retrieval LLM.
code: files <- list.files("docs", pattern = "[.](pdf|docx|html)$", full.names = TRUE) docs  <- docling_convert(files)  corpus <- purrr::imap(docs, \(d, src) {   docling_chunk(d, tokenizer = "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5", max_tokens = 512) |>     docling_embed(embed_api, batch_size = 64) |>     dplyr::mutate(source = src) }) |>   purrr::list_rbind()

title: Extracting tables from documents
text: docling_tables() returns list one tibble per detected table, document order: tibble carries page attribute recording table came :
code: library(doclingr)  doc <- docling_convert("financials.pdf") tables <- docling_tables(doc)  length(tables)      # how many tables Docling found tables[[1]]         # the first table, as a tibble attr(tables[[1]], "page")

title: Extracting tables from documents
text: table model two modes. default "accurate" recovers complex structure (spanning cells, nested headers) cost; "fast" quicker often enough clean grids:
code: doc_fast <- docling_convert("financials.pdf", table_mode = "fast") docling_tables(doc_fast)[[1]]

title: Extracting tables from documents
text: table tibble, whole tidyverse available. example, tag every table page stack one long frame: write table CSV:
code: library(dplyr) library(purrr)  all_tables <- docling_tables(doc) |>   imap(\(tbl, i) mutate(tbl, .table = i, .page = attr(tbl, "page"))) |>   list_rbind()  all_tables tables <- docling_tables(doc) iwalk(tables, \(tbl, i) readr::write_csv(tbl, sprintf("table-%02d.csv", i)))

title: Extracting tables from documents
text: Column types come back character; coerce readr::type_convert() dplyr::mutate(across(...)) know table’s schema. scanned (image-) PDF returns empty tables, make sure OCR (docling_convert(..., ocr = TRUE), default). wide tables split across chunks RAG, docling_chunk() can repeat header row – see vignette("rag").

code: @Manual{,   title = {doclingr: Document Intelligence via 'Docling'},   author = {Andre Leite and Marcos Wasilew and Hugo Vasconcelos and Carlos Amorim and Diogo Bezerra},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/StrategicProjects/doclingr}, }

title: Document Intelligence via Docling
text: Document intelligence R — turn messy PDFs, Office files HTML AI-ready, structured data. doclingr R interface Docling, open-source document-understanding library. brings layout-aware PDF/DOCX/PPTX/HTML parsing, table extraction, OCR RAG-ready chunking R, exposing small, tidy-friendly API built reticulate. R already pdftools, tabulizer, officer, readtext friends, single “document intelligence RAG” package. doclingr aims fill gap: take document, understand layout, extract tables, preserve structure, chunk , hand back ready search embeddings.

title: Document Intelligence via Docling
text: doclingr talks Docling Python package via reticulate. Install backend :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("StrategicProjects/doclingr") library(doclingr) install_docling()      # creates an "r-docling" Python environment # restart R docling_available()    # TRUE

title: Document Intelligence via Docling
code: library(doclingr)  doc <- docling_convert("https://arxiv.org/pdf/2408.09869")  # Export as_markdown(doc)       # layout-aware Markdown as_json(doc)           # structured DoclingDocument as an R list  # Pages and tables docling_n_pages(doc) tables <- docling_tables(doc)   # list of tibbles tables[[1]]  # Figures -> tibble (captions, pages, optional saved images) doc <- docling_convert("paper.pdf", images = TRUE) docling_figures(doc, image_dir = "figures")  # RAG-ready chunks -> tibble chunks <- docling_chunk(doc, max_tokens = 512) chunks$text[1]  # Match your embedding model's tokenizer for accurate budgets chunks <- docling_chunk(doc, tokenizer = "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5", max_tokens = 512)

title: Document Intelligence via Docling
text: doclingr stays provider-agnostic: bring embedding function (API call, local model via reticulate, …) docling_embed() handles batching tidy assembly embedding list-column.
code: embed_openai <- function(txt) {   # your call to an embeddings API -> matrix (one row per text) }  doc |>   docling_chunk(max_tokens = 512) |>   docling_embed(embed_openai, batch_size = 64) #> # adds `embedding` (list-column) and `n_dim` columns

title: Document Intelligence via Docling
text: Docling’s quality comes deep-learning models (layout analysis, TableFormer table-structure model, OCR). R-native equivalent, doclingr binds maintained Python implementation rather reimplementing — strategy used tensorflow, keras spacyr. get upstream parity free; doclingr focuses idiomatic, tidy R surface.

title: Document Intelligence via Docling
text: Actively developed heading CRAN. public API settling may still change 1.0. Contributions issues welcome https://github.com/StrategicProjects/doclingr.

title: Document Intelligence via Docling

what: Is the Docling backend available? — docling_available
title: Is the Docling backend available? — docling_available
text: Checks whether docling Python package can imported active 'reticulate' environment.

title: Is the Docling backend available? — docling_available
code: docling_available()

title: Is the Docling backend available? — docling_available
text: logical scalar.

title: Is the Docling backend available? — docling_available
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ docling_available() } # }

what: Split a document into RAG-ready chunks — docling_chunk
title: Split a document into RAG-ready chunks — docling_chunk
text: Apply Docling chunker converted document return chunks tidy tibble. default "hybrid" chunker produces tokenization-aware, context-enriched chunks well suited embedding retrieval pipelines; "hierarchical" chunker follows document's structural hierarchy without token budget.

title: Split a document into RAG-ready chunks — docling_chunk
code: docling_chunk(   x,   chunker = c("hybrid", "hierarchical"),   tokenizer = NULL,   max_tokens = NULL,   contextualize = TRUE,   ... )

title: Split a document into RAG-ready chunks — docling_chunk
text: x docling_document docling_convert(). chunker Either "hybrid" (default) "hierarchical". tokenizer Hugging Face model id whose tokenizer used count tokens (hybrid chunker ). Defaults small sentence-embedding tokenizer max_tokens set; NULL uses Docling's built-default. max_tokens Optional integer token budget per chunk (hybrid chunker ). NULL, tokenizer's maximum used. contextualize TRUE (default), chunk's text enriched surrounding headings table context via chunker's contextualize() method — form typically embed. raw text always also returned raw_text. ... Additional keyword arguments forwarded Python chunker constructor (example merge_peers repeat_table_header).

title: Split a document into RAG-ready chunks — docling_chunk
text: tibble::tibble one row per chunk columns: chunk_id — 1-based index. text — contextualized text (raw text contextualize = FALSE). raw_text — chunk's unmodified text. n_chars — number characters text. headings — list-column heading paths chunk. pages — list-column integer page numbers chunk spans. n_doc_items — number underlying document items chunk.

title: Split a document into RAG-ready chunks — docling_chunk
text: hybrid chunker token-aware: packs content token budget splits oversized passages. Control tokenizer (model whose tokenizer defines "token") max_tokens (budget). ignored hierarchical chunker.

title: Split a document into RAG-ready chunks — docling_chunk
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ doc <- docling_convert("paper.pdf") chunks <- docling_chunk(doc, max_tokens = 512) chunks$text[1]  # Match your embedding model's tokenizer docling_chunk(doc, tokenizer = "BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5", max_tokens = 512) } # }

what: Convert one or more documents with Docling — docling_convert
title: Convert one or more documents with Docling — docling_convert
text: Runs Docling's document-understanding pipeline local file paths URLs returns lightweight R handles around resulting DoclingDocuments. handle can exported Markdown JSON (as_markdown(), as_json()), mined tables (docling_tables()), split chunks RAG (docling_chunk()).

title: Convert one or more documents with Docling — docling_convert
code: docling_convert(   source,   ocr = TRUE,   table_mode = c("accurate", "fast"),   device = c("auto", "cpu", "cuda", "mps"),   num_threads = NULL,   images = FALSE,   images_scale = 1,   ... )

title: Convert one or more documents with Docling — docling_convert
text: source character vector file paths /URLs. single source returns one docling_document; multiple sources converted one batch returned docling_document_list. ocr Logical; run OCR document. Defaults TRUE. Set FALSE skip OCR faster conversion born-digital documents. table_mode Table-structure model mode, one "fast" "accurate" (default). "accurate" produces better table structure speed cost. device Accelerator device deep-learning models: "auto" (default), "cpu", "cuda", "mps". num_threads Optional integer number CPU threads accelerator. NULL (default) leaves Docling's default. images Logical; generate retain page picture images can saved later docling_figures(). Defaults FALSE (smaller, faster results). Required want image files . images_scale Image resolution scale relative 72 DPI images TRUE (example 2 approx. 144 DPI). Defaults 1. ... Reserved future pipeline options; currently ignored warning supplied.

title: Convert one or more documents with Docling — docling_convert
text: single source, object class docling_document (list underlying Python document original source). multiple sources, docling_document_list: list docling_document objects named source.

title: Convert one or more documents with Docling — docling_convert
text: Supported inputs include PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, Markdown, AsciiDoc common image formats, determined installed Docling version.

title: Convert one or more documents with Docling — docling_convert
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ doc <- docling_convert("https://arxiv.org/pdf/2408.09869") as_markdown(doc)  # Batch, OCR off, fast tables docs <- docling_convert(c("a.pdf", "b.pdf"), ocr = FALSE, table_mode = "fast") } # }

title: Attach embeddings to chunks — docling_embed
text: Embed chunk tibble's text user-supplied embedding function return tibble embedding list-column. doclingr stays provider-agnostic: bring embedder (OpenAI/Cohere/Ollama API call, local sentence-transformers model via reticulate, anything), helper handles batching, validation tidy assembly.

title: Attach embeddings to chunks — docling_embed
code: docling_embed(chunks, embedder, text_column = "text", batch_size = NULL)

title: Attach embeddings to chunks — docling_embed
text: chunks tibble chunks, typically docling_chunk(). embedder function taking character vector returning either numeric matrix one row per input, list equal-length numeric vectors. text_column Name column embed. Defaults "text". batch_size Optional integer; set, embedder called successive batches many texts results concatenated. Useful APIs per-request limits. NULL (default) embeds one call.

title: Attach embeddings to chunks — docling_embed
text: chunks added embedding list-column numeric vectors, n_dim integer column giving embedding's length.

title: Attach embeddings to chunks — docling_embed
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ chunks <- docling_chunk(docling_convert("paper.pdf"), max_tokens = 512)  # Any embedder: here a toy one embed_fn <- function(txt) matrix(stats::runif(length(txt) * 8), nrow = length(txt)) docling_embed(chunks, embed_fn) } # }

title: Export a converted document — docling_export
text: Render docling_convert() result downstream-friendly format.

title: Export a converted document — docling_export
code: as_markdown(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'docling_document' as_markdown(x, ...)  as_text(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'docling_document' as_text(x, ...)  as_html(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'docling_document' as_html(x, ...)  as_json(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'docling_document' as_json(x, ...)  as_doctags(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'docling_document' as_doctags(x, ...)

title: Export a converted document — docling_export
text: x docling_document docling_convert(). ... Additional arguments passed underlying Docling export method (example image_mode Markdown).

title: Export a converted document — docling_export
text: as_markdown(): length-1 character string Markdown. as_text(): length-1 character string plain text. as_html(): length-1 character string HTML. as_json(): R list mirroring DoclingDocument structure. as_doctags(): length-1 character string Docling's DocTags format.

title: Export a converted document — docling_export
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ doc <- docling_convert("report.pdf") as_markdown(doc) str(as_json(doc), max.level = 1) } # }

what: Extract figures (pictures) from a converted document — docling_figures
title: Extract figures (pictures) from a converted document — docling_figures
text: Return tidy tibble pictures Docling detected, captions page numbers. image_dir supplied document converted images = TRUE, picture written disk path returned.

title: Extract figures (pictures) from a converted document — docling_figures
code: docling_figures(x, image_dir = NULL, format = "png")

title: Extract figures (pictures) from a converted document — docling_figures
text: x docling_document docling_convert(). image_dir Optional directory save picture images . Created exist. Requires docling_convert() called images = TRUE; otherwise image data unavailable image_path NA warning. format Image file format saving, example "png" (default) "jpeg".

title: Extract figures (pictures) from a converted document — docling_figures
text: tibble::tibble one row per figure columns: figure_id — 1-based index. caption — caption text (empty string none). page — page number figure appears (NA unknown). image_path — path saved image, NA saved.

title: Extract figures (pictures) from a converted document — docling_figures
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ doc <- docling_convert("paper.pdf", images = TRUE) figs <- docling_figures(doc, image_dir = "figures") figs$image_path } # }

title: Extract tables as data frames — docling_tables

title: doclingr: Document Intelligence via 'Docling' — doclingr-package
text: interface 'Docling', document-understanding library converts 'PDF', 'DOCX', 'PPTX', 'HTML' image documents structured, AI-ready data. package wraps 'Docling' 'Python' package 'reticulate' extract layout-aware text, tables metadata, export 'Markdown' 'JSON', split documents context-rich chunks suitable retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) embedding pipelines.

title: doclingr: Document Intelligence via 'Docling' — doclingr-package
text: Maintainer: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Authors: Andre Leite leite@castlab.org Marcos Wasilew marcos.wasilew@gmail.com Hugo Vasconcelos hugo.vasconcelos@ufpe.br Carlos Amorim carlos.agaf@ufpe.br Diogo Bezerra diogo.bezerra@ufpe.br

what: Install the Docling Python backend — install_docling
title: Install the Docling Python backend — install_docling
text: Installs docling Python package (dependencies) 'reticulate'-managed environment. thin wrapper around reticulate::py_install() typically run installing doclingr.

title: Install the Docling Python backend — install_docling
code: install_docling(   envname = "r-docling",   method = c("auto", "virtualenv", "conda"),   extra = NULL,   ... )

title: Install the Docling Python backend — install_docling
text: envname Name , path , target Python environment. Defaults "r-docling", created first use. method Installation method passed reticulate::py_install(): one "auto", "virtualenv", "conda". extra Optional character vector additional pip/conda specs install alongside Docling (example "docling[ocr]" pinned version "docling==2.0.0"). ... arguments forwarded reticulate::py_install().

title: Install the Docling Python backend — install_docling
text: Invisibly NULL, called side effect.

title: doclingr 0.1.0
text: First release: R interface Docling document intelligence RAG. docling_convert() converts file path URL (vector ) docling_document, pipeline options OCR, table mode, accelerator device/threads image generation. Exporters as_markdown(), as_text(), as_html(), as_json() as_doctags(). docling_tables() returns detected tables list tibbles. docling_figures() returns figure captions/pages can save images; docling_n_pages() reports page count. docling_chunk() produces RAG-ready chunks tidy tibble, hybrid hierarchical chunkers, tokenizer/max_tokens control, pages/ n_doc_items metadata. docling_embed() attaches embeddings user-supplied embedding function, batching. install_docling() / docling_available() manage Python backend.
